title: Certificate Transparency — New Public Cert for Corporate Brand

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ct sourcetype=ct:log earliest=-7d
| where match(names,"(?i)mycompany\.(com|net)") AND !match(names,"\*\.cdn")
| stats earliest(_time) as first by names, issuer
description: Ingests CT log events for certificates issued for company domains to catch shadow issuance.
value: Ingests CT log events for certificates issued for company domains to catch shadow issuance.
grandmaExplanation: Ingests CT log events for certificates issued for company domains to catch shadow issuance — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Allowlist approved CAs; (2) revoke fraudulent; (3) feed brand protection.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Certstream or vendor HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ct:log`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Allowlist approved CAs; (2) revoke fraudulent; (3) feed brand protection.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ct sourcetype=ct:log earliest=-7d
| where match(names,"(?i)mycompany\.(com|net)") AND !match(names,"\*\.cdn")
| stats earliest(_time) as first by names, issuer
```

Understanding this SPL

**Certificate Transparency — New Public Cert for Corporate Brand (DE.CM-05)** — Ingests CT log events for certificates issued for company domains to catch shadow issuance.

Documented **Data sources**: `ct:log`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Certstream or vendor HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ct; **sourcetype**: ct:log. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ct, sourcetype=ct:log, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(names,"(?i)mycompany\.(com|net)") AND !match(names,"\*\.cdn")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by names, issuer** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
